creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.
contentSnippet: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.
contentSnippet: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

title: (No AI) Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_A_3800fc.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Anthropic just [released Claude Fable 5](https://www.theverge.com/news/946725/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-mythos), calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and [praising](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5) its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to [the former flagship model](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/939094/anthropic-claude-4-8-opus-honesty-effort), Claude Opus 4.8.

It isn't because Fable doesn't know the answers. It's because Anthropic won't let it, by design.

Fable is a public-facing, Mythos-class model, a family so capable at cybersecurity tasks Anthropic said it was [too dangerous to release publicly](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917644/anthropic-claude-mythos-breach-humiliation). But while Anthropic has spent much of the [extended Mythos rol …](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917644/anthropic-claude-mythos-breach-humiliation)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/947973/fable-wont-answer-basic-biology-questions)
contentSnippet:
Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship model, Claude Opus 4.8.
It isn't because Fable doesn't know the answers. It's because Anthropic won't let it, by design.
Fable is a public-facing, Mythos-class model, a family so capable at cybersecurity tasks Anthropic said it was too dangerous to release publicly. But while Anthropic has spent much of the extended Mythos rol …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship […]

title: (No AI) Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
author: Mia Sato
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/11/STK095_MICROSOFT_CVirginia_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

New college graduates around the country have been [booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/935602/graduates-boo-ai-ceos). Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out.

In [a blog post](https://blogs.microsoft.com/on-the-issues/2026/06/10/ai-jobs-and-the-next-generation/) running more than 3,100 words, Microsoft vice chair and president Brad Smith addressed the recent spate of viral clips from graduation ceremonies, like former Google CEO Eric Schmidt [getting an earful at the University of Arizona](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tNH43a1EI7s), or the speaker in Florida who [seemed surprised](https://www.youtube.com/shorts/ZCNgENc_0N0) when students booed at the mention of AI as "the next industrial revolution." The videos speak to a broader societal sentiment around AI - the technology [is deeply unpopular](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/891724/nbc-news-march-2026-poll-ai-ice) even as technology compani …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/news/947831/college-speakers-booed-ai-microsoft)

The videos speak to a broader societal sentiment around AI - the technology is deeply unpopular even as technology compani …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) PRC-linked influence operations are targeting AI debates in the US

creator: Dell Cameron
title: (No AI) Wrongful Arrest Exposes Failures in One of the Oldest Police Face-Recognition Tools in the US
dc:creator: Dell Cameron
content: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.
contentSnippet: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.

creator: Fernanda González
title: (No AI) China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
dc:creator: Fernanda González
content: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
contentSnippet: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
